description: AppDynamics - Anzen is a Security focused observability solution(module) which processes MELT(Metrics, Entities, Logs, Traces) in order for the customer to investigate the data in forms of graphs and charts for better understanding and further analysis. This solution was able to record any suspicious activity on the s3 buckets  and s3 objects. The architecture of the product is composed of three microservices, one for discovering entities(cloud resources modelled as json objects inside the FSO store) called discovery collector and a metrics collector which was responsible for creating metrics and events based on AWS Server Access Logging service in combination with AWS Athena  used for filtering the logs. The last microservice was a restAPI server which was responsible for query-ing the object stores from FSO(Full Stack Observability) platform and send the proper data to the UI for displaying the charts.  The collectors used oTel standard to communicate the MELT data to the FSO platform

description:
System Verification Engineer (SVT)

The SVT project is instrumental in ensuring the functionality and reliability of automotive systems, with a specific focus on the Instrument Cluster, Central Display, and Co-Driver Display. SVT engineers play a pivotal role in executing various tests, identifying and addressing system issues, and driving continuous improvement in testing processes to deliver high-quality automotive solutions.
responsibilities:
Conducting System Sanity Test (SST) and Vehicle Smoke Test (VST):

Execute SST and VST procedures to validate the basic functionality and safety of automotive systems.
Participate in test drives to evaluate system performance in real-world scenarios.
Test Plan Execution and Reporting:

Execute designated test plans accurately and efficiently.
Report test results promptly, highlighting any deviations or anomalies observed during testing.
Test Bench and Vehicle Flashing:

Perform daily test bench or vehicle flashing activities using high-level TUT, low-level QFIL, FlashGUI, or fastboot methods.
HUD Configuration:

Configure the Head-Up Display (HUD) settings to ensure optimal visibility and functionality.
Bug Reporting and Verification:

Identify, document, and report software and hardware bugs encountered during testing.
Verify bug fixes and ensure their successful implementation.
Log Collection and Analysis:

Collect logs from various sources such as DLT, CAN, tcpdump, and Blue Pirat.
Analyze log data to diagnose system issues and identify areas for improvement.
Test Case Upgradation:

Update and upgrade test cases within the test plans to align with evolving system requirements and functionalities.
Quality Improvement:

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement measures aimed at enhancing the quality and reliability of testing processes.
Propose and implement innovative approaches to improve testing efficiency and effectiveness.
Hands-on Vehicle Testing:

Gain hands-on experience in vehicle testing, both in stationary environments and during test drives.

description:
PROJECT # 1:  NAB STAREXCHANGE NOVA 
CLIENT	        :  NAB, NATIONAL AUSTRALIA BANK, SYDNEY
DOMAIN     : INVESTMENT BANKING   
ROLE            : SENIOR TEST ENGINEER

NAB has built a portal called StarExchange Nova to provide access to various business critical applications.

NovaFX Trade: Nova is integrated with many 3rd party tools i.e

360t
FXALL
FXC
BLOOMBERG
Murex

The above applications provides user to create the below products.
FX SPOT: The settlement will be T+2 days
FX Forword: The trade will be settled in any future date
FX SWAP: The trade has near leg and Far leg
FX NDF: It deals with Non Deliverable Funds.
FX NDS: It deals with Non Deliverable Funds.
FX Block Trade: We can create more than one block deals.
FX Algo Deals: We can create TWAP and XTX algo orders.

FX Post trade: FXC and FXALL support Post trade deals. If the account is Blocked/Restricted then the order can not be confirmed. In Post trade deals we can split the deals and assign it to settled account to get confirmed Status. 

FX OMS Deals: An order management system (OMS) is an electronic system developed to execute securities orders in an efficient and cost-effective manner. We can Book the OMS deal from FXC by using Benchmark session types.

FXC : Provides best price/Best execution. FXC provides the below session types. Using FXCLIENT we can create below session types trades.
1.	RFS- Real Price for Streaming
2.	Benchmark
3.	Algo-  Algorithmic Deals
4.	Portfolio
5.	Allocations 
6.	MTF
7.	EU MTF
8.	UK MTF

For Post trade deals we need to use FXC Trader.

Nova Blotter: Nova provides 2 types of blotters. 
Actie Blotter: It is used to accept the best price for the deals which are created from the 3rd party. It also allow users to edit, decline the deals.
New Blotter: User can see all the details about the trade in New blotter. You can see the Trade id, Order Id, Murex id ,  status and all regulatory fields once the trade got generated in the new blotter.

Murex : Murex is an integrated trading, risk management, and post-trade processing platform used by financial institutions. It supports the full trade lifecycle including front office trading activities, middle office risk management and compliance, and back office settlement and accounting.


PROJECT # 2:  BRMA APPLICATION 
CLIENT	        :  NAB (NATIONAL AUSTRALIA BANK) SYDNEY
DOMAIN     : INVESTMENT BANKING   
ROLE            : SENIOR TEST ENGINEER

Once the trade got generated from the Upstream we can validate the regulatory fields in the middle office, BRMA Application. 
GWML/ISO? Once the trade created, we can get the GWML from Murex team. Then we need to mask the Gwml.

ISACC : Through ISACC we can Inject the trade by selecting the particular queue. In ISACC we have different queues for different regions.

BRMA?:BRMA is a Regulatory reporting and control application. It contains different regions like ASIC, MAS, CFTC, EMIR etc. 
Once Inject the trade BRMA applications provides all the details of the trade. If Trade is getting processed without any issues it will be called as acknowledged trade. If it is process with any error It will be NACK trade.

GWML/ISO Validation: For each trade we can have the GWML and ISO. The values in GWML and ISO are displaying as per BRMA Application.

description: Deutsche Bank Commercial Banking
responsibilities:
As member of the DB CommBanking Data Squad I was involved in several projects which augmented and improved existing functionality or added new applications:

* Designed (100%) and Implemented (80%) the new Know-Your-Client (KYC) Decision Engine Broker

The new KYC DeBroker microservice role is to replace outdated infrastructure and applications used in supporting the CommBank's KYC decision processes.

The new microservice running on Google GKE was implemented using Java Spring, JPA to persist to a CloudSQL DB, Liquibase for DB Schema maintenance and migration maintenance, Testcontainers for integration test implementation, Github Actions and Helm for deployment, and structured logging for integration with New Relic.

The microservice uses lightweight threads (LWT) to achieve massive internal parallelism (running approx 50 to 100  LWT per vCPU) and adaptive job selection window algorithms together with PostgreSQL row-level locking to synchronize parallel process, achieving a throughput of over 1 million/day/pod KYC requests and almost linear pod horizontal scaling. The follow-up migration to Java21 continuation-based virtual threads shows promise of another 10x improvement in performance.

Data ingress from BigQuery to the CloudSQL operational database is implemented via a GCloud dataflow app using the Apache Beam API, including an extension to the Beam JdbcIO API to support write results in order to implement the dead-letter pattern for rejected records -- dead-letter patterns are not supported by the standard Beam JdbcIO sdk. Several PostgreSQL embedded functions/triggers verify and insure data consistency on import.

The KYC DE Broker ms has been placed in service about a month ago and is functioning error free and without incident since.


* Implemented Kafka Ingestion Pipeline and Transformations for Exposing DB Payment Data to Online Channels (mobile apps, web frontend, ...)
As part of this effort, I designed/implemented a multi-threaded Beam connector (extensions of the Beam UnboundedSource/UnboundedReaded) to the DB Payment REST API (which is sitting on top of Kafka), capable of handling 500 million Kafka messages per day; together with a suite of dataflow flex templates connecting the Kafka Payment endpoint to various services and data sinks (Pub/Sub, CloudSQL, BigQuery).
The whole systems is capable of handling over 500 million payment messages per day.
The new Payment-to-Dataflow infra I implemented is now being offered to all Payment Clients throughout DB as an alternative way of accessing near-real-time payment information.
As part of the effort I also completely re-organized the Squad's development process for dataflow flex templates, as a single centralized GH repository, allowing for code-sharing between templates/pipelines and a centralized flex template store from where reusable templates are made available throughout the bank.
 
* Implemented/Improved Google Cloud Composer Apps for Data Ingress from Google Storage to BQ, Integration with Great Expectation Library for Data Verification and Visualization

* Implemented Several GH Workflows and Actions for Cleaning Up/Sanitizing Existing Pipelines, Added Workflows for Integrating Java Unit and Integration Test in the PR Verification Process

* Assisted Team in Code Reviews, Documentation, Interviews with Job Candidates

description:
Project: Battery Management Controller Software Development for Porsche

In this project, I was involved in the comprehensive development of the Battery Management Controller software for the high-voltage battery box. My primary focus was on the design and implementation of critical input and output interfaces that ensure precise control and monitoring of the battery system.

Key responsibilities included translating system-level requirements into detailed software specifications using IBM DOORS, ensuring traceability and alignment between system functionality and software implementation. This rigorous requirements management facilitated robust and compliant software development.

I employed model-based design methodologies leveraging dSPACE TargetLink for concept development and software generation, which enhanced efficiency and accuracy in the control algorithms. This approach enabled seamless integration between design models and production code, reducing errors and accelerating the development cycle.

The software integration process was managed using version control systems like GitHub and Bitbucket, ensuring streamlined collaboration across distributed teams and maintaining code integrity throughout the project lifecycle.

Additionally, I developed automation scripts in Python to streamline repetitive tasks, including the automated copying of PDX, PDU, and HEX files into flash containers, significantly improving the build and deployment efficiency. I also automated the generation of Excel input files required by the Tessy testing tool, facilitating smoother and faster testing cycles.

This project was executed in collaboration with Senior Consultant Technology & Strategy Services GmbH, deputed at Marquardt Groups, showcasing my ability to work effectively in multi-organizational environments and deliver high-quality software solutions for automotive applications.

description:
This track focuses on teaching participants the principles of securing digital systems and networks from cyber threats. It covers topics such as network security, cryptography, ethical hacking, incident response, and risk management. Participants gains hands-on experience in identifying vulnerabilities, implementing security measures, and mitigating risks. 
• Participate in fundamental courses, participants build a strong foundation in areas such as database design, operating systems, programming in Python and PHP, and client-side technologies.
• Extensive training in Red Hat System Administration, ensuring a command over Linux-based systems.
• Extensive training in networking concepts through Cisco certifications, such as Cisco Internetworking and Cisco Network Associate, and gain expertise in managing Windows systems, including Active Directory and infrastructure. 
• Ethical Hacking and Security Assessment, Firewall Technologies, Incident Handling, and Malware Analysis. Learners develop skills in penetration testing, risk assessment, and compliance, preparing them to proactively identify and address potential threats. 
• Advanced tools and techniques such as SIEM, NGFW, Metasploit Essentials and forensic analysis are integrated to enhance technical acumen for roles like penetration testers or cyber incident responders.


Projects:
• Bug Bounty: Participants analyze a live website to identify vulnerabilities, document findings in a professional bug bounty report, and submit recommendations for remediation.
• Pentesting: A custom virtual machine is provided where participants perform a full penetration test, gain root access, and document the process in a detailed penetration testing report.
• Malware analysis: Participants reverse engineer a malware sample to understand its functionality, behavior, and impact, producing a comprehensive analysis report. 
• Network security:  Participants configure a Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) and implement necessary security measures to fortify a network against potential threats.
• GRC: Participants utilize tools like Burp Suite to create a coverage report and perform a risk assessment, providing recommendations to strengthen the organization's security posture.
• Android: Participants conduct a penetration test on an APK file, identifying vulnerabilities and generating a detailed security assessment report.
• IOS: Participants perform penetration testing on an iOS application, documenting identified issues and providing actionable security recommendations in a professional report.
• DFIR: Participants analyze attack logs using a SIEM solution to determine affected systems, identify attacker actions, credentials compromised, and persistence mechanisms, producing an incident response report.

Challenges:
• ITI CTF Challenge: A Capture The Flag (CTF) competition featuring various scenarios, including penetration testing, reverse engineering, incident response, digital forensics, malware analysis, steganography, and cryptography, aimed at enhancing problem-solving skills and hands-on expertise across multiple cybersecurity domains.

Certificates:
• CCNA Routing and Switching
• EcPPTv2 Certified Professional Penetration Tester

description: Identity services, also known as Identity and Access Management (IAM) solutions, are essential in cybersecurity for managing and securing user identities and controlling access to IT resources. Key capabilities include robust user authentication through Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) and biometric methods, and user authorization via Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC). Single Sign-On (SSO) enhances user experience by allowing access to multiple systems with one set of credentials, while identity federation enables the use of the same credentials across different domains, facilitating collaboration. Access management dynamically adjusts permissions based on user behavior, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Identity governance automates user provisioning and de-provisioning, conducts regular access reviews, and maintains audit logs for security and compliance. Self-service capabilities empower users to manage their identities, reset passwords, and request access to resources, reducing administrative burden. The benefits of IAM solutions include enhanced security by ensuring only authorized access to sensitive resources, improved compliance through strict access controls and detailed audit logs, operational efficiency by streamlining user management processes, and user convenience by simplifying the login process and reducing password fatigue.

description:

description: Here my employer is the largest bank in Eastern Europe. The function of our department was collecting debts from customers. To automate the process we had a system which used an operational database. With these raw data we built data markts which was used by our managers to estimate success of our department in collecting debts and to make business decisions.
responsibilities:
I was responsible for development of the data markts and thair performance optimisation.

These some of my day-to-day tasks:

I dealt with Oracle databese system bugs. If I bump into an obvious bug in Oracle software, I used My Oracle Support to make support requests or to find out best practices how to work around system problems. It could be patching (one-off or PSU) which had to be properly tested in the test environment beforehand.

Through Golden Gate actual data were being pouring into our database. Actual reports requires fresh data so it is vital to have replication running. Its troubleshooting was also my duty.

Our production db server had pretty limited disk space (around 40T) so that I reviewed partitioning of large tables. Some of them I have sorted and compressed without downtime.

Migrantion of the production database from 12.1 to 19.9

To control system's health I built metrics gathering. Concurrent wait events, blocking, workload, critical Oracle errors (ORA-600, ORA-7445), Golden Gate lagging - it was convenient to have all these and others metrics at one place, gathered by Prometheus, displayed in Grafana and stored in PostgreSQL's Timescale DB extension.

Nearly a year ago our team left a DevOps engineer. To kept DevOps going I took over his tasks. We used Jenkins, Git, Jira, Bitbucket and Oracle container databases. I developed Jenkins pipelines on Groovy to make the process of development life cycle automated.

description:
Luxoft is a company that provide its specialists to other international companies. I worked for a well known German bank as an Oracle specialist. It was very interesting to work in a team members of which were spread across the globe.

The warehouses’ main function was forming reports for the German bank regulator.

My duties was support of developer staff to give my hand in solving problems with Oracle databases.
I was also responsible for renewing test environments. Data for tests have prepared properly: reduced and masked.

Here I had a real opportunity to work with Exadata stack of technologies like smart scan, storage indexes, hybrid columnar compression.

My employer was a retail chain which sold food through its shops spread across two macro-regions: Ural and West Siberia. Here I’ve got an exciting opportunity to advance my Oracle skills because most of the time I was an only DBA responsible for a quite big environment that included a central data warehouse and more than 200 databases (one per store) which were copying aggregated data into the central one. Apart from these there was an Oracle e-Business Suite 11.5.10 (OeBS) – Oracle supplied ERP system. Plus the warehouse and OeBS had their testing and reporting counterparts. Of course automatic alerting was configured to let me know when something goes wrong like: failed backup, free space is about run out, media failure, and so on.

Media failures of shop databases happened here and there quite often so that database recovery and control of their backup were one of my primary responsibilities.

Among other things I successfully fought against performance degradation. It has to be admitted that the warehouse was projected to serve less amount of shops than my employer had - it by far exceeded its limits. Moreover some code – PL/SQL packages – was not allowed to be changed. It was developed, wrapped and sold to my employer as is by another company. Therefore in these cases I extensively used such tools like: statistics gathering (including histograms), use of outlines, indexing, reorganisation of tables, tuning session and instance parameters. It was predominantly a realm of Oracle 9.2 so I had that pretty limited kit comparing with the situation today. For instance, I partitioned tables when I saw that it brings advantage to have it split into smaller ones that made it  more manageable, and IO pressure could be eliminated within one or more smaller segments.

I assisted developers to optimise performance of report queries and PL/SQL blocks by making analyse of code, query plans, or traces. After thorough investigation I made my recommendation what to do to improve its performance or eliminate its impact on the system.

Another my responsibility was Oracle e-Business Suite cloning which was quite complicated process containing lot’s of steps and possibilities that something went wrong.

To summarise information about the environment:
-- main database (over 1T, Sun Solaris);
-- ERP - Oracle e-Business Suite (Oracle 9i, 150G, Sun Solaris);
-- 2 regional bases (200G or so, Windows -- over 200 databases per store of the retail chain (from 6 to 80G,
8i/9i/10g, Windows).
All bases were run in 24/7.

Over relatively short period of my occupation in this firm I dealt with migration of data from one database to another which had different structures. Our client had their old working system which they want to replace by software developed by my employer. I had to develop SQL scripts which let data be transformed and  transferred to new system.
responsibilities: Development procedures to convert DB schemas.

description: My main responsibility was support of billing system owned by MTS (leading  telecommunication company of the region - SITRONICS’ key partner).

Working for this company I was able to configure replication - transferring of data between several Oracle databases by using materialized views (Oracle Advanced replication).
To make the process of configuration automatic I developed scripts.
Apart from this I also implemented installation and configuration of Oracle software on Windows and Solaris platforms.
I was the only administrator and worked in close cooperation with PHP developers fixing problems connected with Oracle databases.
responsibilities:
Database configuration
Development of scripts on PL/SQL
